Wigandia sylvatica, also known as 'Huacapuranga', is a shrub or small tree with medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential health benefits such as antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Wigandia sylvatica include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
